main.name: INTJ
main.title: The Architect
main.shortDesc: INTJs are strategic, independent thinkers who excel at turning complex ideas into actionable plans. They are driven by a relentless pursuit of knowledge and long-term goals.
main.quote: The measure of intelligence is the ability to change.

infos.title: Overview
items: INTJ stands for Introverted, Intuitive, Thinking, and Judging.
They are one of the rarest personality types, making up about 2% of the population.
INTJs are visionaries who combine creativity with logic to solve complex problems.
They prefer working alone or in small groups and value efficiency above all.
Often described as the most independent of all types, they trust their own judgment deeply.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Open-minded to new ideas backed by evidence
Excellent at identifying patterns and systems

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Can appear arrogant or dismissive of others' opinions
Struggles with emotional expression and empathy
May be overly critical of themselves and others
Can be stubborn when they believe they are right

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ENFP – brings spontaneity and warmth that balances INTJ's intensity
ENTP – shares the love for debate and intellectual exploration
INFJ – mirrors INTJ's depth and long-term vision
ENTJ – aligns on goals, logic, and ambition

main.name: INTP
main.title: The Logician
main.shortDesc: INTPs are inventive thinkers with a passion for theoretical problems and abstract ideas. They value logic above all and are constantly seeking to understand how things work.
main.quote: The important thing is not to stop questioning. Curiosity has its own reason for existence.

infos.title: Overview
items: INTP stands for Introverted, Intuitive, Thinking, and Perceiving.
They make up roughly 3% of the population and are known for their analytical minds.
INTPs love exploring ideas and theories more than practical application.
They are often quiet in social settings but highly expressive in intellectual discussions.
They tend to question everything and rarely accept ideas without thorough examination.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Strong ability to spot inconsistencies and flaws in reasoning
Able to think deeply about complex topics

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Can be absent-minded and lose track of practical responsibilities
May come across as condescending when explaining ideas
Tends to second-guess and overcomplicate decisions
Can be insensitive to others' feelings

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ENTJ – provides the direction and execution that INTP often lacks
ENFJ – offers warmth and social engagement that balances INTP
INFJ – shares depth and intellectual curiosity
ESTJ – grounds INTP's theories with practicality

main.name: ENTJ
main.title: The Commander
main.shortDesc: ENTJs are bold, strategic leaders who thrive at organizing people and resources to achieve ambitious goals. They are natural executives with high standards for themselves and others.
main.quote: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.

infos.title: Overview
items: ENTJ stands for Extraverted, Intuitive, Thinking, and Judging.
They make up about 2% of the population and are often found in leadership roles.
ENTJs have a natural drive to lead and are highly goal-oriented.
They are confident decision-makers who are not afraid of conflict or confrontation.
ENTJs thrive in structured environments and love turning big visions into reality.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Excellent at delegating and organizing teams

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Can be domineering or impatient with others
May struggle to acknowledge their own emotions
Can come across as cold or ruthless in pursuit of goals
Tendency to be intolerant of inefficiency
May overlook the emotional needs of those around them
Can be overly aggressive in competitive situations

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: INFP – brings depth, creativity, and values that balance ENTJ's drive
INTP – offers logical insight and innovation to ENTJ's plans
ENFP – adds enthusiasm and people skills to ENTJ's vision
INTJ – shares strategic thinking and ambition

main.name: ENTP
main.title: The Debater
main.shortDesc: ENTPs are quick-witted, innovative thinkers who love challenging ideas and exploring new possibilities. They thrive on intellectual debate and rarely accept the status quo.
main.quote: I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.

infos.title: Overview
items: ENTP stands for Extraverted, Intuitive, Thinking, and Perceiving.
They make up about 3% of the population and are known for their sharp wit.
ENTPs are energized by debate, brainstorming, and exploring unconventional ideas.
They enjoy playing devil's advocate and often argue positions just to examine them.
ENTPs are highly adaptable and get bored easily with routine or repetitive tasks.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Open to new ideas and perspectives
Excellent at seeing connections others miss
Energetic and enthusiastic in group settings

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Struggles to follow through on projects
May ignore others' feelings in pursuit of being right
Prone to getting bored and losing interest
Has difficulty with routine and structure

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: INFJ – provides depth, warmth, and grounding for the ENTP
INTJ – matches ENTP's intellectual drive with strategic focus
ENFJ – brings emotional intelligence and structure to the relationship
INFP – offers creativity and values that complement ENTP's energy

main.name: INFJ
main.title: The Advocate
main.shortDesc: INFJs are rare, empathetic idealists who are deeply committed to helping others and making the world a better place. They combine intuition with deep moral conviction.
main.quote: Darkness cannot drive out darkness; only light can do that. Hate cannot drive out hate; only love can do that.

infos.title: Overview
items: INFJ stands for Introverted, Intuitive, Feeling, and Judging.
They are the rarest personality type, making up only about 1–2% of the population.
INFJs are deeply intuitive and can often sense people's emotions and motivations.
They are driven by a strong sense of purpose and a desire for meaningful impact.
Despite being introverted, INFJs care deeply about the well-being of others.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Visionary thinking with a focus on the future
Strong sense of integrity and values
Insightful and highly perceptive of others
Dedicated and determined when pursuing goals

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Prone to burnout from overgiving and perfectionism
Can be overly private and hard to get to know
May have difficulty with conflict and confrontation
Can be stubborn about their core beliefs

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ENTP – challenges INFJ's thinking while appreciating their depth
ENFP – shares idealism and passion for people and ideas
INTJ – mirrors INFJ's intuition and long-term thinking
INFP – connects through shared values and desire to help others

main.name: INFP
main.title: The Mediator
main.shortDesc: INFPs are imaginative, empathetic idealists guided by their deep personal values. They seek meaning in everything and aspire to make the world a more authentic and compassionate place.
main.quote: Not all those who wander are lost.

infos.title: Overview
items: INFP stands for Introverted, Intuitive, Feeling, and Perceiving.
They make up about 4% of the population and are known for their rich inner worlds.
INFPs are deeply idealistic and always looking for the good in people and the world.
They are guided by their personal values and seek authenticity in all relationships.
Creative and imaginative, INFPs often express themselves through art, writing, or music.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Passionate about causes they believe in
Strong sense of personal values and ethics

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Can be overly idealistic and impractical
Tends to take criticism very personally
Can be withdrawn and difficult to know
Difficulty focusing on mundane or repetitive tasks

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ENTJ – provides structure and drive that complements INFP's creativity
ENFJ – shares INFP's care for people and meaningful connections
INTJ – brings focus and logic that helps ground INFP's visions
INFJ – shares depth, empathy, and a desire for meaning

main.name: ENFJ
main.title: The Protagonist
main.shortDesc: ENFJs are charismatic, empathetic leaders who are passionate about inspiring and uplifting the people around them. They are natural mentors with a gift for bringing out the best in others.
main.quote: The best way to find yourself is to lose yourself in the service of others.

infos.title: Overview
items: ENFJ stands for Extraverted, Intuitive, Feeling, and Judging.
They make up about 2% of the population and are known as natural-born leaders.
ENFJs are deeply invested in the emotional well-being of those around them.
They are highly social and thrive in collaborative, people-centered environments.
ENFJs are often the glue that holds groups together through their warmth and vision.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Highly empathetic and attuned to others' needs
Deeply loyal and supportive to loved ones

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Can be overly idealistic about people
Tends to neglect their own needs to help others
May struggle with making tough decisions that hurt others
Can be overly sensitive to conflict
Prone to taking on too much responsibility

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: INFP – connects deeply through values and empathy
INTP – balances ENFJ's emotion with logical insight
INFJ – shares vision, depth, and care for people
ISFP – brings creative, easygoing energy to complement ENFJ

main.name: ENFP
main.title: The Campaigner
main.shortDesc: ENFPs are enthusiastic, creative free spirits who see life as full of exciting possibilities. They are deeply people-oriented and love inspiring others to live authentically.
main.quote: You only live once, but if you do it right, once is enough.

infos.title: Overview
items: ENFP stands for Extraverted, Intuitive, Feeling, and Perceiving.
They make up about 7% of the population, one of the more common types.
ENFPs are energetic, optimistic, and genuinely excited by people and ideas.
They are known for their ability to connect deeply with others quickly.
ENFPs dislike routine and are always seeking new experiences and inspiration.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Creative and full of innovative ideas
Optimistic and inspiring to those around them
Curious and open to new experiences

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Can struggle with focus and follow-through
Easily distracted by new ideas before finishing old ones
Prone to overthinking and emotional sensitivity
Can be disorganized and poor at time management
May be overly idealistic or naive
Struggles with highly structured or routine environments

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: INTJ – provides depth, structure, and focus that grounds ENFP
INFJ – shares idealism and emotional depth
ENTJ – brings leadership and direction to ENFP's energy
INTP – connects with ENFP through intellectual curiosity

main.name: ISTJ
main.title: The Logistician
main.shortDesc: ISTJs are responsible, dependable individuals who value tradition, order, and honesty. They are thorough and meticulous in their work, making them exceptionally reliable in any environment.
main.quote: Well done is better than well said.

infos.title: Overview
items: ISTJ stands for Introverted, Sensing, Thinking, and Judging.
They are one of the most common types, making up about 12% of the population.
ISTJs are known for their strong sense of duty and commitment to their responsibilities.
They prefer clear rules, structure, and well-established systems.
ISTJs are private individuals who take their word and commitments very seriously.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Strong work ethic and sense of responsibility
Excellent at creating and following systems

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Can be overly rigid and resistant to change
May appear cold or emotionally distant
Tendency to be judgmental of those who don't follow rules
Struggles with creative or abstract thinking
Can be overly focused on the past and tradition
May have difficulty accepting alternative viewpoints

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ENFP – brings spontaneity and warmth that loosens ISTJ's rigidity
ESFP – adds fun and social energy to ISTJ's structured life
ISFJ – shares values of duty, loyalty, and commitment
ESTJ – aligns on structure, responsibility, and logic

main.name: ISFJ
main.title: The Defender
main.shortDesc: ISFJs are warm, caring individuals who are deeply committed to protecting and supporting the people they love. They are humble, reliable, and go above and beyond for others.
main.quote: No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.

infos.title: Overview
items: ISFJ stands for Introverted, Sensing, Feeling, and Judging.
They make up about 13% of the population, one of the largest groups.
ISFJs are highly observant and remember small details about the people they care for.
They value harmony, tradition, and maintaining close, stable relationships.
Despite being introverted, ISFJs have a strong desire to be of service to others.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Good memory for details, especially about people
Practical and grounded in their approach

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Tends to suppress their own needs to please others
Can be overly modest and underestimate themselves
Difficulty saying no and setting boundaries
Resistant to change and new approaches

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ESTP – brings excitement and action that balances ISFJ's stability
ESFP – adds energy and social warmth to ISFJ's caring nature
ISTJ – shares values of duty, loyalty, and structure
INFJ – connects through shared care for others and long-term thinking

main.name: ESTJ
main.title: The Executive
main.shortDesc: ESTJs are disciplined, practical organizers who excel at managing people and systems. They uphold tradition, value order, and are dedicated to getting things done the right way.
main.quote: Order is not pressure which is imposed on society from without, but an equilibrium which is set up from within.

infos.title: Overview
items: ESTJ stands for Extraverted, Sensing, Thinking, and Judging.
They make up about 9% of the population and are natural managers and administrators.
ESTJs value rules, loyalty, and clear expectations in all aspects of life.
They are direct communicators who prefer facts over theories or emotions.
ESTJs are highly organized and take great pride in being dependable and efficient.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Reliable and consistent in all they do
Excellent at creating and enforcing systems
Good at managing and motivating teams

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Can be inflexible and resistant to change
May come across as bossy or overbearing
Struggles with abstract or theoretical thinking
Can be too focused on rules at the expense of context
May be judgmental of those who don't meet their standards
Difficulty acknowledging emotions in themselves and others

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ISFP – brings creativity and openness that softens ESTJ's rigidity
ISTP – shares a practical, logical approach to problem-solving
INFP – provides emotional depth and values to balance ESTJ's focus
ISTJ – aligns fully on structure, duty, and responsibility

main.name: ESFJ
main.title: The Consul
main.shortDesc: ESFJs are caring, sociable individuals who thrive on creating harmony and supporting the people around them. They are warm hosts and devoted friends who take their commitments seriously.
main.quote: Too often we underestimate the power of a touch, a smile, a kind word, a listening ear.

infos.title: Overview
items: ESFJ stands for Extraverted, Sensing, Feeling, and Judging.
They are one of the most common types, making up about 12% of the population.
ESFJs are highly attuned to social dynamics and the needs of those around them.
They love bringing people together and creating a sense of community.
ESFJs are practical helpers who show love through action and care.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Excellent team player and community builder
Practical and good at getting things done
Very attentive to the needs of others

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Can be overly concerned with social approval
May struggle with confrontation and conflict
Tends to put others' needs above their own
Can be inflexible about traditions and expectations
May be too sensitive to criticism
Difficulty adapting when social dynamics change

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ISFP – shares warmth and appreciation for harmony
ISTP – brings calm, practical problem-solving to the relationship
INFP – connects through shared empathy and care for others
ISFJ – aligns on loyalty, support, and community values

main.name: ISTP
main.title: The Virtuoso
main.shortDesc: ISTPs are hands-on problem solvers who thrive in exploring how things work. They are calm under pressure, highly observant, and prefer action over discussion.
main.quote: Do not pray for an easy life, pray for the strength to endure a difficult one.

infos.title: Overview
items: ISTP stands for Introverted, Sensing, Thinking, and Perceiving.
They make up about 5% of the population and are known for their mechanical aptitude.
ISTPs are independent and private, preferring to observe before acting.
They are highly adaptable and thrive in unpredictable, hands-on environments.
ISTPs are rational, efficient, and often excellent in a crisis.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Great at understanding how systems and machines work
Flexible and adaptable to changing situations

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Can appear cold or emotionally unavailable
Gets bored easily with long-term commitments
Can be stubborn and resistant to direction
May have difficulty maintaining long-term relationships

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ESFJ – brings warmth and social connection to ISTP's reserved nature
ESTJ – provides direction and structure that ISTP may lack
ENFJ – adds emotional depth and relational skills
ENTJ – aligns on logic and practical decision-making

main.name: ISFP
main.title: The Adventurer
main.shortDesc: ISFPs are gentle, creative souls who live in the moment and seek beauty in everything around them. They are deeply in touch with their senses and express themselves through art and action.
main.quote: The purpose of art is washing the dust of daily life off our souls.

infos.title: Overview
items: ISFP stands for Introverted, Sensing, Feeling, and Perceiving.
They make up about 9% of the population and are known for their artistic sensibilities.
ISFPs are quiet but highly expressive through their chosen art form or craft.
They are deeply empathetic and caring, though they keep their feelings private.
ISFPs love freedom, spontaneity, and prefer to go with the flow.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Deeply empathetic and sensitive to others
Charming and easy to be around
Lives fully in the present moment
Passionate and dedicated to personal values

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Highly sensitive to conflict and criticism
Can be unpredictable and difficult to plan with
Can be overly private and hard to read
Difficulty expressing their deeper thoughts and feelings

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ENFJ – provides vision and structure that supports ISFP's creativity
ESFJ – shares warmth and care for others in a complementary way
ESTJ – grounds ISFP with practicality and direction
ENTJ – challenges ISFP to grow beyond their comfort zone

main.name: ESTP
main.title: The Entrepreneur
main.shortDesc: ESTPs are bold, energetic thrill-seekers who are at their best when taking action. They are perceptive, charming, and excel at reading people and adapting to any situation.
main.quote: Life is either a daring adventure or nothing at all.

infos.title: Overview
items: ESTP stands for Extraverted, Sensing, Thinking, and Perceiving.
They make up about 4% of the population and are known for their bold, direct nature.
ESTPs are risk-takers who love fast-paced environments and immediate results.
They are highly observant and skilled at reading people and social dynamics.
ESTPs are pragmatic problem-solvers who prefer to learn by doing.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Excellent people skills and social intelligence
Quick thinkers who adapt rapidly to new situations
Energetic and motivating to those around them
Practical and resourceful in a crisis

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Tends to be insensitive to others' emotions
May take risks without thinking of consequences
Gets bored easily in routine environments
May struggle with long-term planning and commitments

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ISFJ – brings warmth and reliability that grounds ESTP
ISTJ – provides structure and follow-through ESTP often lacks
INFJ – adds depth and emotional intuition to ESTP's dynamic energy
ISFP – connects through a shared love of living in the present

main.name: ESFP
main.title: The Entertainer
main.shortDesc: ESFPs are spontaneous, energetic performers who love life and people. They live fully in the present moment and bring excitement and warmth wherever they go.
main.quote: To live will be an awfully big adventure.

infos.title: Overview
items: ESFP stands for Extraverted, Sensing, Feeling, and Perceiving.
They make up about 9% of the population and are natural entertainers.
ESFPs are vibrant, fun-loving individuals who enjoy being the center of attention.
They are deeply people-oriented and have a natural talent for making others feel included.
ESFPs are practical and prefer hands-on experience over theoretical learning.

infos.title: Strengths
items: Spontaneous, fun, and full of energy
Highly sociable and easy to connect with
Observant and attuned to others' emotional needs
Enthusiastic and encouraging to those around them
Practical and quick to act in the moment

infos.title: Weaknesses
items: Can struggle with long-term focus and planning
Easily bored by routine or repetitive tasks
May avoid difficult or uncomfortable topics
Can be overly sensitive to conflict
Tends to be impulsive with decisions
May struggle with academic or highly structured environments

infos.title: Compatible Types
items: ISTJ – provides stability, structure, and reliability for ESFP
ISFJ – shares warmth, care, and a love of helping others
INTJ – brings depth and long-term thinking that complements ESFP
ISTP – connects through shared practicality and love of action
